definition.q: Abortion is defined as termination of pregnancy before:
definition.explain: Abortion is termination of pregnancy before viability of the fetus, i.e. before 20 weeks.

definition.q: The most common cause of early spontaneous abortion is:
definition.explain: Chromosomal abnormalities account for approximately 50% of early abortions.

definition.q: Which of the following best defines this condition?
options: A pathological deviation from normal anatomy or function
An incidental finding with no clinical significance
definition.explain: A definition describes the essential nature of a condition as a deviation from normal.

definition.q: The etiology of this condition involves:
options: A complex interplay of genetic, environmental, and multifactorial elements
definition.explain: Most obstetric conditions have multifactorial etiology.

definition.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
definition.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

management.q: Which factor most influences the choice of treatment?
options: All of the above — treatment is individualized
management.explain: Obstetric management is tailored to gestational age, maternal status, and available resources.

management.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
management.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

options: Depends on the specific condition and severity
management.explain: Management approach varies — some conditions require surgery, others medical or conservative care.
